name: Elderflower Pear Water Kefir
subtitle: Delicate, elegant probiotic soda with elderflower cordial and ripe pear
blrNote: Elderflower cordial is available at Nature's Basket and Foodhall in Bengaluru (Belvoir, Bottlegreen brands, ₹400–600 per bottle) or on Amazon India. A bottle lasts many batches — you need only 2 tablespoons per batch. Indian pears (nashpati) are available year-round at KR Market and Hopcoms (₹80–150/kg). This is an elegant, sophisticated flavour that works well for dinner parties. At BLR temps, 24 hours of 2F is enough.

IN.where: Homemade from water kefir grains. See water-kefir-basic recipe.

ingredients.name: Elderflower cordial
substitutions: Dried elderflowers (1 tbsp) steeped in warm water

IN.where: Nature's Basket, Foodhall, Amazon India. Belvoir or Bottlegreen brands. ₹400–600/bottle (lasts many batches).

ingredients.name: Ripe pear (chopped)
substitutions: Apple — similar sugar content, different flavour

IN.where: KR Market, Hopcoms, fruit vendors. Indian and imported pears available year-round. ₹80–150/kg.

equipment.name: Plastic or nylon mesh strainer
equipment.notes: Fine mesh to strain out pear pieces.

tldr: Add elderflower cordial and chopped ripe pear to finished water kefir, seal and ferment 24–48 hours. A delicate, elegant, floral-fruity probiotic soda.

steps.title: Prepare base water kefir
steps.instruction: Make a batch of plain water kefir following the water-kefir-basic recipe. Strain out the grains. You need about 750ml of finished plain water kefir.
tips: For this delicate flavour, aim for a milder 1F — less tang, more neutral. A 24-hour 1F works best.
Reserve grains in fresh sugar water for your next batch.
steps.checkpoint: 750ml of strained, room-temperature plain water kefir

steps.title: Add elderflower cordial and pear
steps.instruction: Chop 1 medium ripe pear into small pieces (skin on — it adds tannins and flavour). Add the chopped pear and 2 tablespoons of elderflower cordial to your swing-top bottle. The cordial already contains sugar, so additional sugar is usually unnecessary.
tips: Ripe, fragrant pears work best — they should yield slightly when pressed.
Bartlett, Anjou, or Indian nashpati all work well.
The elderflower cordial provides both flavour and sugar for carbonation.
steps.checkpoint: Chopped pear and elderflower cordial in the bottle

steps.title: Second ferment (2F)
steps.instruction: Pour the strained water kefir over the pear and elderflower mixture. Seal tightly. Place in a warm spot away from direct sunlight for 24–48 hours. Burp every 12 hours. The pear provides moderate sugars for gentle carbonation.
tips: This is a gentler ferment than fruit-heavy flavours like mango or pineapple.
The liquid will take on a pale golden hue with a beautiful floral aroma.
At BLR temps, 24 hours gives gentle carbonation. 48 hours for more fizz.
The elderflower aroma is more pronounced when served chilled.
steps.checkpoint: Bottle lightly pressurized, pale golden, floral aroma

steps.title: Strain and serve
steps.instruction: Open carefully. Strain through a fine mesh strainer to remove pear pieces. Serve chilled in a wine glass or champagne flute — this is an elegant drink. Refrigerate for up to 5 days.
tips: Serve in a wine glass or flute — this rivals Prosecco in elegance.
Excellent as a non-alcoholic aperitif at dinner parties.
A thin pear slice on the rim makes a beautiful garnish.
The flavour is subtle and sophisticated — pair with light appetizers.
steps.checkpoint: Pale golden, delicately floral, gently fizzy probiotic soda

culturalContext.story: Elderflower is deeply woven into European culture — from English elderflower champagne to Scandinavian elderflower cordials to Austrian Holunderblütensirup. Pears have been cultivated in Europe for over 3,000 years. Combining them in a water kefir creates a drink that feels like a summer afternoon in the English countryside — but with probiotics.
culturalContext.historicalNote: The elder tree (Sambucus nigra) was considered sacred across European folklore — it was believed to ward off evil spirits. Elderflower champagne and cordials have been made in English country houses for centuries. Perry (fermented pear juice) has been brewed in England since Roman times.
culturalContext.significance: This recipe shows water kefir's versatility — it can be rustic and bold (ginger beer) or refined and elegant (elderflower pear). It's an excellent choice for converting wine and champagne drinkers to probiotic beverages.
culturalContext.funFact: Elderflower season in Europe lasts only 2–3 weeks in late May/June. The entire year's supply of elderflower cordial is made during this brief window. One of the reasons elderflower cordial is expensive in India — it's imported from a very short harvest.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Elderflower cordial is the key ingredient
thingsToAccountFor.description: The quality of your elderflower cordial determines the drink. Belvoir and Bottlegreen are good options available in India. Avoid very cheap cordials with artificial flavouring — look for real elderflower extract on the label.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Subtle flavour can be overwhelmed
thingsToAccountFor.description: Elderflower and pear are both delicate flavours. If your 1F is very tangy, it may overpower them. Use a milder (shorter) first ferment for best results.

dehydratorIntegration.method: Not recommended for flavoured water kefir.
tips: Freeze into ice cubes for adding to sparkling water — instant elderflower pear fizz

variations.name: Elderflower Lemon Kefir
variations.description: Replace pear with the juice of 1 lemon. Classic elderflower lemonade, now probiotic and fizzy.

variations.name: Elderflower Apple Kefir
variations.description: Replace pear with 100g chopped apple. More available and affordable in India — Shimla or Kinnaur apples work beautifully.
